def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    rsi_window = params['rsi_window']
    atr_window = params['atr_window']
    threshold_multiplier = params['threshold_multiplier']
    df['rsi'] = ta.momentum.rsi(df['close'], window=rsi_window)
    df['atr'] = ta.volatility.average_true_range(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=atr_window)
    df['upper_threshold'] = df['close'] + threshold_multiplier * df['atr']
    df['lower_threshold'] = df['close'] - threshold_multiplier * df['atr']
    signals = pd.Series(np.where((df['close'] > df['upper_threshold']) & (df['rsi'] > 70), -1, np.where((df['close'] < df['lower_threshold']) & (df['rsi'] < 30), 1, 0)), index=df.index)
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: GBP/JPY exhibits momentum reversal tendencies where price tends to reverse after breaching volatility-adjusted bands confirmed by RSI overbought/oversold conditions.
